articleRattanThickness.meta_description: How to pick artificial rattan thickness and profile for furniture: load zones, cross-section shape, color batching, and yield.
articleRattanThickness.title: Choosing rattan thickness for furniture
articleRattanThickness.lead: A practical take: profile shape, structural load, and keeping color consistent across coils.
articleRattanThickness.intro: Artificial rattan is sold by the coil and priced by weight: thicker profiles are usually stiffer and less bendy. For furniture, the millimetres on the spec sheet matter together with the cross-section — half-round, flat, round, oval — and whether the weave carries body load (seat, arm) or is decorative trim.
articleRattanThickness.sec1_a: Areas that bear body weight — seats, loaded arms — usually need a thicker or stiffer profile with even geometry. Decorative rings, overlays, and fine mesh can use thinner material where neat weaving matters more than bending resistance on a long span.
articleRattanThickness.sec1_b: If you hesitate between two neighbouring thicknesses for a large surface, test sag on a mock-up with the same geometry. On a long span a couple of millimetres is more noticeable than on a short run.
articleRattanThickness.sec2_a: Half-round is common for classic shapes and radii; flat suits flat planes and ribbon-style weaving; round works for fine repeating coils. Oval changes pitch and pattern — read shape and thickness together on the product card.
articleRattanThickness.sec2_b: Outdoors, confirm UV stabilization and allowed conditions for the SKU; indoors, color consistency across coils on repeat orders is usually the bigger risk.
articleRattanThickness.sec3_a: One batch number for the whole job reduces two-shade mismatches in one piece. Estimate yield by coil length and overlap; a short trial piece on your actual weaving setup validates both flexibility and real consumption.
articleRattanThickness.sec3_b: Start with a small trial coil when you change series or supplier — you check bend feel and whether the gauge matches your fixtures.
articleRattanThickness.outro: In the Bententrade catalog each item lists thickness, profile shape, color family, and stock. Need a matched set of profiles for a chair or table? Send span, load, and a sketch via contacts — we’ll advise from workshop experience.
articleRattanThickness.image_1_alt: Rattan profile types from one production batch
articleRattanThickness.image_1_caption: Compare shape and gauge side by side before launching a repeatable furniture series.
articleRattanThickness.image_2_caption: The twisted relief creates a deeper visual pattern and hides minor wear traces.

articlePlantersOutdoor.meta_description: How to choose outdoor planters: UV stability, drainage setup, volume, and maintenance workflow for terraces and facades.
articlePlantersOutdoor.title: Outdoor planters: UV and drainage done right
articlePlantersOutdoor.lead: A practical brief for terraces and entrance zones: pick the right volume, material behavior, and service routine so planters stay neat through the season.
articlePlantersOutdoor.intro: For outdoor installations, shape and color are only part of the result. Long-term appearance depends on UV stability, proper drainage, and a realistic maintenance routine. If these points are fixed early, the composition lasts longer and needs fewer emergency fixes.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec1_h: UV stability and color in direct sun
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec1_a: Terrace planters often stay in full sun for hours every day, so UV-stabilized material is the baseline. It helps keep color behavior predictable and reduces visible mismatch between older and newly added units.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec1_b: If you expect phased expansion, lock the color family and batch logic upfront. It keeps the visual line coherent when adding more planters later.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec2_a: Even high-quality planters lose look quickly when water stagnates. Basic setup: drainage holes, a drainage layer, and an inner planting volume sized to the root system.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec2_b: For commercial spaces, unified inner containers across one series speed up maintenance and make plant replacement easier without reworking the whole composition.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec3_a: For facade and traffic zones, 10–16L formats often balance visibility and plant mass best. They read clearly from distance and hold stable composition.
articlePlantersOutdoor.sec3_b: Plan maintenance from day one: watering schedule, dust cleaning, and drainage checks. A simple routine reduces unplanned visits and keeps the whole line consistent.
articlePlantersOutdoor.outro: When requesting an outdoor set, include zone size, sun exposure, and desired spacing. That makes volume and model selection faster and avoids overbuying.
articlePlantersOutdoor.image_1_alt: Outdoor planter series in a linear composition
articlePlantersOutdoor.image_1_caption: Repeatable forms and equal spacing create a clean visual rhythm in commercial and residential zones.
articlePlantersOutdoor.image_2_caption: On bright sides, UV-stable material behavior becomes especially visible over time.

articleWholesaleTimelines.meta_description: Wholesale and HoReCa timeline: brief structure, batch reservation, paperwork, and dispatch checkpoints.
articleWholesaleTimelines.title: Wholesale and HoReCa: timelines and delivery stages
articleWholesaleTimelines.lead: A practical step-by-step flow that keeps project deadlines realistic: what to lock in the brief, when to reserve batch, and how to prepare shipping docs.
articleWholesaleTimelines.intro: Most B2B delays happen before production: incomplete specs, unconfirmed volume, or late document requirements. A structured kickoff brief removes these risks early.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec1_a: At intake, lock profile type, color family, quantity per item, and the target on-site date. If the project has phases, list dispatch order as well.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec1_b: For HoReCa and network sites, appoint one decision owner per side. It cuts approval loops and keeps communication linear.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec2_a: After spec approval, reserve color batch for the full scope. This is critical when part of the volume ships later and visual continuity matters.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec2_b: For larger loads, decide whether dispatch is one wave or split waves. This helps align storage, transport windows, and installation planning.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec3_a: Before shipping, verify invoice package and specification set required by your procurement flow. Confirm consignee contacts and receiving window at the same step.
articleWholesaleTimelines.sec3_b: A final 24-hour check on package list and labels reduces partial-delivery risk and accelerates acceptance on-site.
articleWholesaleTimelines.outro: The core rule is simple: the more precise the initial brief, the more stable the deadline and budget outcome. For larger projects, send structure early.
articleWholesaleTimelines.image_1_caption: Labeling and spec cross-check before shipment reduce intake friction on the client side.
articleWholesaleTimelines.image_2_alt: Commercial order staged by project phases
articleWholesaleTimelines.image_2_caption: Phase-based staging simplifies installation and supports predictable handover windows.

home.strip_outdoor: Patios, winter gardens, humid rooms — the polymer doesn’t swell like cellulose-based materials.
home.strip_care: Dust and marks — soft damp cloth; skip acetone and harsh solvents.

wholesale.title: Wholesale
wholesale.lead: Made in Tashkent: artificial rattan yarn and hand-woven planters, 18 colors, several profile types. Classic yarn wholesale from 5 kg; volume discounts on request. Separate flow from retail, stable color batches, and a PDF quote after volumes are confirmed.
wholesale.benefit_1_body: We align color and series so the whole project batch looks consistent — for workshops and HoReCa alike: fewer rejects and rework on the floor.
wholesale.benefit_2_body: Invoice, specification, and supporting docs in the standard flow; extra formats for legal entities and export under contract.
wholesale.benefit_3_body: Volumes and timelines are handled outside the retail queue — faster alignment, nationwide shipping in Uzbekistan, and clear dispatch stages.
wholesale.step1: Request: volume (kg or planter lines), profile type, colors from the palette, and target on-site date.
wholesale.step2: We align batch and samples; one batch number for the whole order when volume allows.
wholesale.step3: Invoice, payment as agreed, shipment from Tashkent with supporting documents.
wholesale.form_note: Submissions go to our API; with CRM webhook env vars they’re also forwarded to your stack.
wholesale.cases: References for HoReCa, floral, and furniture buyers — ask your manager.

export_page.title: Export B2B
export_page.lead: Artificial rattan and hand-woven planters from Uzbekistan: USD/EUR invoices, logistics, and duty ballparks — calculated for destination country, Incoterms, and cargo mix.
export_page.checklist_title: What we align before the invoice
export_page.checklist_1: Incoterms and handover point — they drive insurance and liability on the route.
export_page.checklist_2: Line items and gross weight — for customs description and container or truck choice.
export_page.checklist_3: Currency and payment form — prepayment or documents against payment as agreed.
export_page.checklist_4: Production lead time and color batch hold — fixed before invoicing.
export_page.quote_checkout: Quote and calculation first — payment after terms are approved.
export_page.form_note: Fields for orientation; final quote and invoice follow from the manager.

about.title: Production
about.lead: For over three years we have produced artificial rattan yarn and woven planters in Tashkent: a wide palette, batch control, and predictable color on reorders.
about.meta_description: Bententrade: Tashkent production, artificial rattan and planters, batch control, color stability, and legal details for contracts.
about.quality_body: Verified feedstock and batch checks; extra verification for HoReCa and dealers on request. Production and warehouse under one roof — less color drift between shipments; thousands of orders across Uzbekistan.
about.batch_body: Every batch is labeled. For wholesale — one batch number per order where volume allows; 18 colors in the range for series matching.
about.requisites_body: Contract and invoice details are sent after a request to opt@bententrade.uz or via phone / Telegram manager.
